# codex-swarm v0.7.0

`v0.7.0` turns coordination state into an operational control plane: Codex
tasks can be indexed across hosts, grouped into logical operations, inspected
for open loops, and supported by durable decision records. App-server workers
also launch asynchronously without running Codex under a privileged daemon.

## Highlights

- `cs tasks ingest|list|status` keeps a durable host-scoped task inventory with
  stable pagination, unread state, coordinator classification, and explicit
  missing or tombstone records.
- `cs tasks collect page|status|finish` lets a host resume paged snapshot
  collection with exact cursor chains, bounded staging, idempotent replay, and
  deliberate `window` or `complete` coverage semantics.
- `cs attention` derives the actionable open loops already present in SQLite,
  including queued messages, blocked claims, stale workers, failed gates, and
  pull-request next actions.
- `cs operation list|show` groups coordination evidence under canonical issue
  or root-worker keys while keeping broken ancestry visible as degraded data.
- `cs decision record|list|show|supersede` preserves rationale, evidence,
  dissent, author provenance, and atomic supersession history.
- `cs spawn --engine appserver` returns after durable host, thread, turn, and
  worktree readback while the first turn continues asynchronously.

## Runtime and safety contract

The machine-global SQLite ledger remains authoritative. Host-owned collectors
observe Codex tasks; they do not scrape undisclosed history, start app-server,
or invent lifecycle transitions. Complete-snapshot omission is the only mode
that marks an indexed task missing, and tombstones remain explicit.

Privileged Windows LocalSystem or Unix root daemons refuse to launch Codex.
When needed, `cs` starts a listener-free runtime under the interactive caller's
identity, sharing the same SQLite ledger without opening a competing daemon
listener. Parent timeouts and closed response pipes do not convert an already
created task into a failed or duplicate task.

GitHub Actions used to build this release are pinned to canonical commit SHAs.
Release archives remain provider-neutral and include Windows version metadata;
the Windows binaries are intentionally not Authenticode-signed.

## Upgrade

Replace `cs` and `csd` as a matched pair and restart the existing daemon. The
SQLite schema migrates in place; keep the daemon's current loopback address and
state path. Verify the upgrade with `cs version`, `cs doctor`, and `csd status`.
